name: Morning Trap Runner — SBR Daily KPI

nodes.name: 6AM Daily Trigger
nodes.notes: Fires at 6:00 AM daily (MST). Zo checks traps first thing.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ query: `SELECT campaign.name, campaign.status, metrics.cost_micros, metrics.conversions_value, metrics.conversions, metrics.clicks, metrics.impressions, metrics.interactions, metrics.interaction_rate, metrics.cost_per_conversion FROM campaign WHERE segments.date BETWEEN '${new Date(new Date().getFullYear(), new Date().getMonth(), 1).toISOString().split('T')[0]}' AND '${new Date(Date.now() - 86400000).toISOString().split('T')[0]}' AND campaign.status = 'ENABLED' ORDER BY metrics.cost_micros DESC` }) }}

nodes.name: Google Ads — MTD Campaigns
nodes.notes: Pulls MTD campaign data via Google Ads API. Zo tracks: spend, conv value (sales), ROAS, conversion rates per campaign. He noted 6 active campaigns, some at 30:1 ROAS, newer ones at 10:1. MTD example: $3,200 spend / $38-43K sales.

nodes.name: Amazon Ads — MTD Performance
nodes.notes: Pulls Amazon Advertising data. Zo tracks: attributed sales ($70K/30d example), ad spend ($3,400), ACOS vs TACOS. TACOS includes organic traffic driven by Google/FB/TikTok spillover. He noted conversion rates and traffic volume.

nodes.name: Shopify — MTD Orders + Analytics
nodes.notes: START HERE — credential already configured. Pulls MTD orders from Shopify. Zo checks: gross sales, order count, checkout conversion rate (usually 20-30%), return rate, YoY comparison. Also tracks source breakdown (direct, Amazon, TikTok, 'no referrer' anomaly).

nodes.name: Claude — Morning Briefing
nodes.notes: Claude analyzes all 3 data sources and produces Zo's morning SMS briefing. Uses SBR brand voice. Flags anomalies (Zo noted checkout conversion dropped from ~27% to 16%, return rate was up, 'no referrer' traffic anomaly). Under 400 words.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ type: 'SMS', contactId: $vars.ZO_GHL_CONTACT_ID, message: $('Claude — Morning Briefing').item.json.content[0].text }) }}

nodes.name: GHL SMS — Send to Zo
nodes.notes: Sends the morning briefing to Zo via GHL SMS. Replaces his manual Voxer copy-paste routine. Uses GHL Conversations API v2. Requires: ZO_GHL_CONTACT_ID (from sbr-product-marketing-context.md), GHL Location API key with conversations.message.write scope.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ run_date: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0], google_ads_raw: $('Google Ads — MTD Campaigns').item.json, amazon_ads_raw: $('Amazon Ads — MTD Performance').item.json, shopify_order_count: $('Shopify — MTD Orders + Analytics').all().length, shopify_gross_sales: $('Shopify — MTD Orders + Analytics').all().reduce((sum, o) => sum + parseFloat(o.json.total_price || '0'), 0), claude_briefing: $('Claude — Morning Briefing').item.json.content[0].text, sms_sent: true, sms_sent_at: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.name: Supabase — Log Run
nodes.notes: Logs the full trap run to Supabase for historical tracking. Table: morning_trap_runs. Stores raw API responses, computed totals, Claude's briefing text, and SMS delivery status. This gives Zo a searchable history of every morning check.
